item.headline: Member of Technical Staff @ Thinking Machines Lab
item.about: Somewhat deep, somewhat sequential, and somewhat implicit.

multiLocaleHeadline.headline: Member of Technical Staff @ Thinking Machines Lab

experience.description: Audio-visual modeling, self-supervised learning, generative models, synthetic data, multimodal LLMs. Reality Labs Research + GenAI Llama pretraining research.

Starting in Aug 2017, I am a full-time Ph.D. student at Carnegie Mellon University (Machine Learning Department of SCS), under the supervision of J. Zico Kolter. My research interests are in deep learning architecture and some interesting optimization+DL problems. I was also the coordinator of the CMU AI Seminar (http://www.cs.cmu.edu/~aiseminar/).

I defended my doctoral thesis “Equilibrium Approaches in Deep Leaning” and graduated in May 2022!

I was a teaching assistant for CMU’s course 10-701: Intro to Machine Learning (PhD), working with Prof. Manuela Veloso and Prof. Pradeep Ravikumar. This graduate course covers a broad range of advanced topics in Machine Learning.
I joined the Facebook AI Research (FAIR) as a PhD research intern in summer 2020, working with Michael Auli.

In summer 2019, I worked at Robert Bosch LLC (the US office *and* the Germany office) as a machine learning research intern, under (my advisor and Bosch’s chief AI scientist) Zico Kolter. Parcipated in the Kaggle competition "Predicting Molecular Properties" with the BCAI team and was ranked 1/2749 (top 0.05%).
In summer 2018, I worked with Vladlen Koltun at Intel Intelligent Systems Lab (AI lab) as a graduate research intern.

In spring 2017, I worked (again) with professor Mahadev Satyanarayanan (Satya) and Padmanabhan Pillai (Babu) as a teaching assistant in the course 15-440/640: Distributed Systems. This was an undergrad & grad level course that covered the fundamentals about the modern Distributed Systems (RPC, caching, MapReduce, VM, Scaling, Security, etc.).
I worked as one of the teaching assistants for CMU's graduate-level machine learning course, 10-601, taught by Prof. Roni Rosenfeld in fall 2016.
Worked as a software engineer intern at PayPal's Merchant Onboarding team on its (then) latest detection machine learning (ML) project.

I worked as a teaching assistant of CMU's 15-440/640 Distributed Systems class with professor Mahadev Satyanarayanan (Satya) and Padmanabhan Pillai (Babu) in S16. This was an undergrad & grad level course that covered the fundamentals about the modern Distributed Systems (RPC, caching, MapReduce, VM, Scaling, Security, etc.).

I worked for a lunar project for an Astrobotic program that dealt with a mission sending objects to the moon in 2015, along with faculty in Carnegie Mellon. My primary job was to search through the database, and through discussion, helped determine the objects to be sent to the Moon.

I was the TA of course 33-131 Matters and Interactions I at Carnegie Mellon. In class I taught some materials that the instructor did not get into details or cover in lecture, and helped my students with their doubts and practice problems.

I worked as a software developer summer intern at Epic Systems. Cooperating with two other college interns, we sought to build a new generation web-based Epic's patient registration system based on the existing kiosk prototype called Welcome. The whole application was built mainly using ASP.NET MVC 5 with Razor and Entity Framework, with development environment connected to Epic's own database. The new app would enable a very efficient identification narrowing, appointment check-in, demographics verification, various payment method and online document signing with an even smarter workflow configuration. The programming languages involved are C#, HTML, CSS and Javascript (including the use of jQuery library, like ajax). I also used Objective-C and managed to convert this web-application to an iOS8 app that runs on an iPad.

education.description: I joined CMU's Machine Learning Department in School of Computer Science as a Ph.D. student in fall 2017. I worked on deep learning architectures, sequence modeling, and implicit networks under the supervision of Professor J. Zico Kolter.
Graduated with University Honor and Mellon College of Science (MCS) College Honor.

Graduated with University Honor and School of Computer Science (SCS) College Honor. My undergraduate senior thesis is "The Effect of Pre-ReLU Input Distribution on Deep Neural Net's Performances", advised by Professor J. Zico Kolter.

honorsAndAwards.title: Dean's List with High Honor of School of Computer Science, Mellon College of Science and Dietrich College
honorsAndAwards.description: This high honor is awarded to students with extraordinary academic record at CMU. Being in an interdisciplinary program at CMU, I have been awarded such high honor with recognition from two colleges (SCS, MCS and DC) in all semesters since Fall 2013.
